ddls:
CREATE TABLE "transactions" (
  "transaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each transaction */,
  "transaction_name" TEXT /* Name of the transaction (e.g., accessControl_Transaction) */,
  "sla_status" TEXT /* SLA status of the transaction (e.g., Show SLA Results) */,
  "transaction_type" TEXT /* Type of transaction (e.g., API call, database query, file transfer) */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* ID of the system or application the transaction belongs to */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environment (e.g., development, testing, production) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the transaction was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last update to the transaction record */,
  "description" TEXT /* A brief description of the transaction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("transaction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "transaction_id" INTEGER /* ID of the transaction the metric belongs to */,
  "minimum" REAL /* Minimum response time for the transaction */,
  "average" REAL /* Average response time for the transaction */,
  "maximum" REAL /* Maximum response time for the transaction */,
  "std_deviation" REAL /* Standard deviation of response times for the transaction */,
  "percent_90" REAL /* 90th percentile response time for the transaction */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the performance metrics were measured */,
  "throughput" REAL /* Throughput of the transaction (e.g., transactions per second) */,
  "success_rate" REAL /* Percentage of successful transactions */,
  "failure_rate" REAL /* Percentage of failed transactions */,
  "error_rate" REAL /* Percentage of transactions that resulted in errors */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the performance metrics were first recorded */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last update to the performance metrics */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_transaction_id FOREIGN KEY ("transaction_id") REFERENCES transactions ("transaction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sla_thresholds" (
  "threshold_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each SLA threshold */,
  "transaction_id" INTEGER /* ID of the transaction the threshold belongs to */,
  "metric" TEXT /* Performance metric the threshold applies to (e.g., minimum, average, maximum) */,
  "threshold_value" REAL /* Threshold value for the metric */,
  "threshold_type" TEXT /* Type of threshold (e.g., warning, critical) */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* ID of the system or application the threshold belongs to */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environment (e.g., development, testing, production) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the SLA threshold was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last update to the SLA threshold */,
  "description" TEXT /* A brief description of the SLA threshold */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("threshold_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sla_thresholds_transaction_id FOREIGN KEY ("transaction_id") REFERENCES transactions ("transaction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sla_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each SLA result */,
  "transaction_id" INTEGER /* ID of the transaction the result belongs to */,
  "pass" INTEGER /* Number of times the transaction passed the SLA threshold */,
  "fail" INTEGER /* Number of times the transaction failed the SLA threshold */,
  "stop" INTEGER /* Number of times the transaction was stopped */,
  "sla_status" TEXT /* SLA status of the transaction (e.g., Show SLA Results) */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* ID of the system or application the result belongs to */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environment (e.g., development, testing, production) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the SLA results were measured */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the SLA result was first recorded */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last update to the SLA result */,
  "description" TEXT /* A brief description of the SLA result */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sla_results_transaction_id FOREIGN KEY ("transaction_id") REFERENCES transactions ("transaction_id")
)
